body_paragraphs: Two macro stories are colliding eight days before the Fed meets, and neither was on the radar a week ago. The US has bombed Iran for nine straight nights, oil is up roughly 20% this month, and the market's response has been to slash hike odds from a near coin flip to 13 percent - cheaper money and expensive oil arriving at the same time, which is not usually how this works.

Locally, your two summer storylines split in opposite directions today. The Marlins' cushion, built on a 20-6 June, is gone - Max Meyer's hurt, the skid is somewhere between four and six games depending who's counting, and Houston isn't offering a reset. Hafley's Dolphins, meanwhile, stopped being theoretical this morning: rookies reported to Baptist Health, which means the patience-year talk now has actual practice film behind it.

Beyond the feed: China Grill is back at Bal Harbour after a decade away, and a two-week expedition off Brazil just catalogued 31 species no one had seen before. Neither will move a market. Both are more interesting than most of what will this week.

read_only_three.label: The rabbit hole: what's actually happening with Iran

what_changed.current_state: CME FedWatch now shows an 86.7% probability of a hold; hike odds down to 13.3%, even as the 10Y ticked up to 4.59%.
what_changed.why_it_matters: The debt-cost math for the second half just moved further and faster than the inflation data alone explains.

themes.title: The war repricing the Fed
themes.thesis: A month-old conflict just became the biggest variable in Fed week.
themes.synthesis: The US has struck Iran for nine consecutive nights after the deaths of three American service members, and Iran has retaliated against tankers in the Strait of Hormuz - the Houthis followed Monday by declaring their own blockade against Saudi Arabia. Brent is up roughly 20% since June to $89.22, WTI to $83.23. Mediators are floating a 10-day ceasefire; the White House has not committed to one.
themes.new_today: Trump vowed Iran would pay for the deaths of three service members; oil extended its climb and dragged the S&P down with it Monday.
themes.why_it_matters: Energy costs feed straight into the inflation print the Fed is watching eight days out - which is likely why hike odds just collapsed rather than rose.

sources.headline: Oil prices rise after Trump says Iran will pay for killing U.S. service members
US completes more strikes against Iran after Trump warning to Tehran
2026 Iran war

themes.title: Hike odds collapse to 13%
themes.thesis: The coin-flip hike Alex was tracking last week is now a rounding error.
themes.synthesis: CME FedWatch put a July hike at 46.5% eight days ago; as of Monday it is 13.3%, with an 86.7% probability the Fed holds at 3.50-3.75%. Inflation itself has not cooled - CPI is still 4.2%, PCE 4.1% - so the repricing looks driven by the Iran-oil shock and the accompanying equity wobble rather than better data. The 10Y ticked up anyway, to 4.59%, as oil pushed inflation expectations higher even while hike bets fell.
themes.new_today: Hike odds fell from a coin flip to a long shot in a single week - the sharpest repricing of this cycle.
themes.why_it_matters: This is the input that reprices CRE debt costs and cap rates for the second half of the year, and it just moved further and faster than the inflation data alone would explain.

sources.headline: Will the Fed Cut Rates in July 2026? Here's What the Markets Say
S&P 500 closes slightly lower on Monday, weighed down by rising oil prices
CME FedWatch Tool

themes.title: The skid keeps going in Houston
themes.thesis: The cushion built in June is gone, and Houston is not offering a reset.
themes.synthesis: Miami lost the series opener to the Astros 7-4 Monday, extending a slide that started before the All-Star break - some accounts put it at six straight games and counting. All-Star right-hander Max Meyer is out with a neck strain, and shortstop Otto Lopez has missed back-to-back games. The record has slipped toward .500 territory in a race the Marlins were leading three weeks ago.
themes.new_today: Monday's 7-4 loss extended the skid, with Meyer now on the injured list.
themes.why_it_matters: This was supposed to be the summer of the first real Marlins playoff race in years; two more losses in Houston and it stops being one.

sources.headline: Marlins vs. Astros box score, July 20 2026
Astros vs. Marlins: Injury Report, Updates and Probable Starters - July 20

themes.title: Hafley's first camp is real now
themes.thesis: The patience-year talk stops being theoretical the moment pads go on.
themes.synthesis: Rookies reported to the Baptist Health Training Complex this morning; veterans follow July 28. The first public practice is Aug 2, with a joint session against the Giants on Aug 20 and one open practice at Hard Rock Stadium on Aug 8 - all free. It is the sixth straight year Baptist Health has hosted camp.
themes.new_today: Camp officially opened today with rookies reporting.
themes.why_it_matters: This is the start of the actual evidence for or against a Hafley rebuild that has so far only existed on paper.

sources.headline: Dolphins announce schedule for 2026 Training Camp presented by Baptist Health
Dolphins Reveal Public Training Camp Schedule in Full

themes.title: Three clocks, one summer
themes.thesis: Miami's title-track teams are filling in the roster math while the patience-year team just starts practicing.
themes.synthesis: The Heat's post-Giannis roster is ten players deep with $20.5M under the hard cap for five spots left to fill; sportsbooks moved their title odds from 30-1 to 18-1. The Panthers wrapped development camp in Fort Lauderdale, locked up a forward on an eight-year extension, and open the 2026-27 season on the road against Cup champion Carolina. Neither is urgent reading tonight - both are worth knowing before the season starts.
themes.new_today: The Panthers released their 2026-27 schedule; the Heat's cap situation is now public.
themes.why_it_matters: Knowing the Heat and Panthers are both win-now while the Dolphins are still finding their roster tells you how differently to watch each of your teams this year.

sources.headline: Heat Make Roster Move Eight Days After Giannis Antetokounmpo Trade
Florida Panthers Announce 2026-27 Regular Season Schedule

themes.title: The summer table, now open
themes.thesis: The anticipated list from three days ago is starting to become an actual reservation list.
themes.synthesis: China Grill is back - not in South Beach as first reported, but at Bal Harbour Shops, with the old lobster pancakes and lamb spare ribs alongside new dishes. Suite Habana opened a new South Beach location, Stand (from the Shingo team) is running breakfast and lunch on Miracle Mile with an izakaya dinner service coming this fall, and a Toronto hospitality team took over the old Planta Queen space in Coconut Grove. La Sponda, Manoli, and PopUp Bagels are still ahead this summer.
themes.new_today: Several of the anticipated openings are now confirmed open, including China Grill's actual location.
themes.why_it_matters: The eating map keeps redrawing itself while the city's attention was on the World Cup - worth catching before these tables get hard to book.

sources.headline: 9 most anticipated Miami restaurants opening summer 2026
New restaurants are opening across Miami. Here are some of the latest

themes.title: Control what's actually yours
themes.thesis: The oldest Stoic drill is also the most useful one for a week this loud.
themes.synthesis: New academic writing on Stoicism keeps circling back to the same mechanism: separating what you can control from what you cannot is not a mood, it is a practiced skill. It is a useful lens for a week where a war, a Fed reversal, and a losing streak all showed up within 48 hours, none of which change what is actually in front of you today.
themes.new_today: Nothing urgent - this is standing practice, not news, which is the point.
themes.why_it_matters: Your operating philosophy, applied to an unusually loud news week instead of an abstract one.

sources.headline: Applying Philosophy of Stoicism for Psychological Control in Recent Complex Society
Articles

themes.title: What 31 new species say about noticing
themes.thesis: The most interesting discoveries this month are happening somewhere no one is watching.
themes.synthesis: A Schmidt Ocean Institute expedition spent two weeks off Brazil and came back with 31 previously unknown deep-sea species, found in the midwater zone between sunlit surface and seafloor. Separately, researchers expanded the Global Ocean Gene Catalog by more than 50% - over 500 million newly catalogued genes - and found evolution runs markedly faster in the deep ocean than near the surface. NOAA begins a 28-day expedition to the Cook Islands seabed this week.
themes.new_today: The gene-catalog and species findings both published within the past two weeks.
themes.why_it_matters: A small, deliberate reminder that most of what is actually happening in the world is not on anyone's feed - the literal version of the noticing habit you already practice.

sources.headline: Scientists discovered 31 new deep-sea species off Brazil
Deep-sea life has a secret food source scientists never expected
Ocean Exploration in the Field: 2026

rabbit_hole.title: The 2026 Iran War
rabbit_hole.creator: Britannica editors
rabbit_hole.why: Markets just repriced the Fed around this conflict without most coverage explaining how it got here - the strikes, the Strait of Hormuz, and the collapsed June ceasefire in one sitting. Twenty-five minutes now will make every market story this week easier to read.

beautiful_thing.title: 1111 Lincoln Road
beautiful_thing.creator: Herzog and de Meuron
beautiful_thing.body: A seven-level parking garage in Miami Beach that refuses to look like one: raw concrete floors of varying height, no exterior walls, and enough structural drama that it now hosts weddings, fashion shows, and a ground-floor restaurant instead of just cars. Herzog and de Meuron - the Swiss firm behind Tate Modern and the Bird's Nest stadium - treated a utilitarian building type as a chance to make public sculpture you can drive through. It remains one of the only parking structures ever discussed seriously as architecture.

noticing_prompt.text: Notice the exact moment the light changes color before today's afternoon storm arrives - it happens fast, and almost nobody watches it happen on purpose.

evening_quote.text: If you seek tranquility, do less.

evening_unwind.evening_prompt: What today actually required your full effort, and what only felt like it did?

quote.text: It is not that we have a short time to live, but that we waste a lot of it.
